g.data-narration: Swing revolutionized Java desktop development with lightweight components and powerful event handling.
g.text: Why Swing Matters in GUI Development

g.data-narration: Swing introduces two fundamental architectural patterns that every developer must master.
The Delegation Event Model uses observer pattern where components delegate event handling to specialized listener objects.
g.text: A design pattern where event sources delegate event handling to listener objects instead of polling.

g.data-narration: The three key components are: Event Source generates events, Event Object carries event data, Event Listener processes them.
Here's how event registration works in practice - you register listeners with the component to handle specific events.
Swing events form a hierarchy rooted in java.util.EventObject, with specialized events extending AWTEvent.
MVC separates application logic into three layers: Model for data, View for presentation, Controller for input handling.
g.text: Separates application logic into three distinct layers for maintainability and modularity.

g.data-narration: Swing implements a simplified MVC called Separable Model Architecture where UI delegates handle the view and controller.
g.text: Swing uses a simplified MVC where ComponentUI handles View, and models manage data state.

g.data-narration: Different Swing components use specific model types to manage their state and data structures.
Pluggable Look and Feel (PLAF) allows changing the visual appearance of Swing applications without modifying code.
g.text: Swing applications can dynamically change their visual appearance at runtime.

g.data-narration: Swing components are not thread-safe. All UI updates must occur on the Event Dispatch Thread (EDT) for safety.
g.text: Swing components are NOT thread-safe. All UI updates must happen on the EDT.
WRONG: Direct updates from background thread

g.data-narration: These architectural patterns power real-world applications from trading platforms to hospital management systems.
Remember these key points: event sources delegate to listeners, MVC separates concerns, and always update UI on EDT.
For exams: remember the three event components, MVC model types for components, and always use EDT for UI updates.
g.text: • Know the three event components: Source, Event, Listener
• Remember component model types: JTable→TableModel, JList→ListModel
• All UI updates MUST use EventQueue.invokeLater() for thread safety
